parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces significant new attack surface — retry logic, BigModel error dispatch, job tracking, background execution, and CI quality gates — without visible evidence that critical failure paths (concurrent state mutation, retry idempotency, partial write recovery) have been validated under adversarial conditions. The deferred cleanup of `process.mjs` (deleted entirely) and heavy refactors to `glm-client.mjs`, `state.mjs`, and `tracked-jobs.mjs` raise integration-risk concerns that existing UAT scenarios (v0.4.4, v0.4.5 only) do not cover. I cannot confirm ship-readiness from the context alone.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs with no clear migration audit for former callers
findings.body: The diff shows `scripts/lib/process.mjs` as a removed file (67 lines deleted). If any runtime path or third-party integration still imports from this module, it will produce an unhandled `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` at runtime — a silent-time-bomb failure that won't appear until the code path is exercised in production. No test file named `process.test.mjs` appears in the test suite, suggesting the removal was not guarded by regression coverage.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase (including node_modules entry points, plugin manifests, and any dynamic import paths) for references to `process.mjs` or `lib/process`. If any remain, either restore the module with a deprecation shim or add a regression test that asserts no import errors on all subcommands.

findings.title: New retry.mjs module lacks visible idempotency guarantees for side-effectful API calls
findings.body: `scripts/lib/retry.mjs` is a new 203-line module (with 352 lines of tests, which is good). However, retry logic applied to BigModel/GLM API calls that have side effects (e.g., job submission, state transitions) can cause duplicate operations if the server processed the request but the response was lost. The `bigmodel-errors.mjs` dispatch layer maps error codes to retry decisions, but without a demonstrated idempotency-key mechanism or request deduplication, retries risk creating duplicate jobs or double-applying state changes. The tracked-jobs module may partially mitigate this, but no test evidence demonstrates concurrent-retry safety.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration-level test that simulates a 'server processed but client timed out' scenario: submit a job, mock a network timeout on response, verify that retry does not create a duplicate. Document the idempotency contract (or lack thereof) in the module header.

findings.title: state.mjs refactor may break concurrent CLI invocations sharing state file
findings.body: `scripts/lib/state.mjs` is modified (32 lines changed). If state is persisted to a shared filesystem path (likely given the CLI nature), concurrent invocations of the companion tool could race on reads/writes without file-level locking. The tracked-jobs module (`tracked-jobs.mjs`, 78 lines changed) likely depends on atomic state updates. No test file exercises concurrent process scenarios based on the file list.
findings.recommendation: Verify whether state.mjs uses atomic writes (write-to-temp + rename) or advisory locking. Add a concurrent-access test: two processes writing state simultaneously, assert no data loss or corruption. If using naive JSON read-modify-write, this is a real corruption risk.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs refactor expands error surface without visible edge-case coverage for auth failure modes
findings.body: `scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs` has 155 lines changed — a substantial refactor. Combined with the new `bigmodel-errors.mjs` dispatch, this expands the error-handling surface. Auth failures (expired tokens, permission errors) may now be retried unnecessarily if classified as transient. If a 401/403 is misclassified as retryable, the backoff loop will burn through retry attempts before failing, delaying user feedback and potentially triggering rate limits.
findings.recommendation: Audit the error-classification table in `bigmodel-errors.mjs` to confirm HTTP 401/403/status-code auth errors are explicitly classified as non-retryable. Add a unit test verifying that authentication errors fail immediately without retry.

next_steps: Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD -- scripts/lib/process.mjs` to confirm no live importers remain; if any do, this is a ship-blocker.
Execute the existing test suite (`npm test` or equivalent) and verify all 352 lines of retry tests and 189 lines of bigmodel-errors tests pass without flak.
Conduct a focused adversarial test: invoke the CLI with an invalid/expired API key and verify it fails fast without retrying (auth-error non-retryable invariant).
Inspect state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs for file-locking or atomic-write patterns; document findings before release.
